mechanism_narrative: The 3-month trend in FEDFUNDS is used continuously: increases in the policy rate raise equity discount rates and reduce risk appetite, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month FEDFUNDS changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward observations or Rank IC is above zero.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above a restrictive threshold, equity valuations face a higher hurdle rate and financing-sensitive earnings expectations decline, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if triggered returns are not below untriggered returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS indicates stronger household demand and near-term revenue momentum for listed firms, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when retail sales growth is positive.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS changes are followed by lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than negative RSAFS changes over a 3-year rolling walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals deteriorating labor income and recession risk, causing analysts to mark down earnings expectations and predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the trigger is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered cases or if mean triggered returns are not below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth is positive while UNRATE also rises, payroll expansion is failing to absorb labor-market slack, signaling weaker earnings quality and predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is above 0 and UNRATE 3-month change is above 0.2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-positive and UNRATE-rising condition has no lower 60-day return than PAYEMS-positive and UNRATE-stable periods across walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A positive short-term trend in INDPRO signals improving real output and operating leverage for cyclical firms, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO 1-month changes are followed by non-positive average 5-day S&P 500 returns or Rank IC is below 0 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong HOUST indicates forward construction demand, bank credit creation, and durable-goods spending, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when starts exceed an expansion threshold.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST above 1500 is followed by lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than HOUST below 1500 in more than half of walk-forward folds.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is rising while FEDFUNDS is already high, inflation persistence prevents policy relief and keeps real financing conditions restrictive, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month change is above 0.6% and FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-CPI and high-FEDFUNDS condition does not produce lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than high-FEDFUNDS alone in rolling walk-forward tests.
